message:
ci(bench): label software-fallback / no-adapter runs honestly

The published bench summary was reporting llvmpipe (GitHub Actions
Linux's CPU-software Vulkan emulator) and Windows-without-D3D12
runs as if they were real GPU numbers. A reader scanning the
table would see Linux Vulkan-direct = 255 ms / 4× and conclude
the implementation is slow, when actually the runner has no GPU
at all and the real Iris Xe number is ~25 ms / 32× per HANDOFF.md.

Two changes:

1) bench-summary.mjs probes the GPU device at start, classifies as
   `real-gpu` / `software-fallback` (llvmpipe / lavapipe / swiftshader
   / software) / `no-adapter`, and writes both into bench-results.json.
   Per-OS step summary now leads with the device line so anyone
   reading a single OS report knows what hardware they're looking at.

2) bench-aggregate.mjs reads device_kind from each artifact and:
   - prints a "Device per runner" table at the top so the audience
     sees "Linux: ⚠️ software CPU (llvmpipe)" before any number;
   - restricts the headline "vs ORT Node CPU" speedup table to
     real-GPU rows only — software-fallback comparisons are CPU
     vs CPU and don't represent the value prop;
   - keeps the per-T detail table for all rows so software runs
     are still visible, just clearly tagged.

Real-hardware numbers (M2 Pro Metal, Iris Xe Vulkan) live in
HANDOFF.md and the docs. CI numbers should not be cited as if
they're those.
